leadText: Lycanroc (Japanese: ルガルガン Lugarugan) is a Rock-type Pokémon introduced in Generation VII. It evolves from Rockruff starting at level 25. The form Rockruff evolves into depends on the time, as well as its Ability, which corresponds to one of Rockruff's two visually indistinct forms. Rockruff with the Abilities Keen Eye, Vital Spirit, or Steadfast evolve into: Midday Form Lycanroc when leveled up during the day. Midnight Form Lycanroc when leveled up at night. Rockruff with the Ability Own Tempo evolve into Dusk Form Lycanroc when leveled up during the evening. (Specifics may differ in past games. Refer to Game data→Evolution data for these details.)

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Contents 1 Biology 1.1 Forms 1.1.1 Midday Form 1.1.2 Midnight Form 1.1.3 Dusk Form 1.2 Evolution Biology [ edit | edit source ] Lycanroc is a canine Pokémon resembling a wolf. It has three forms: Midday Form, Midnight Form, and Dusk Form. The first two forms are influenced by Rockruff being bathed in Solgaleo or Lunala solar or lunar energy. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] The third form is said to have been influenced by the setting sun of the evening. Lycanroc are used by humans as guards to protect their fields from Deerling . Lycanroc (in its Midday and Dusk Forms) is the only known Pokémon capable of learning the move Accelerock . It is the only known Pokémon capable of using the Z-Move Splintered Stormshards . Forms [ edit | edit source ] Lycanroc has three forms: Midday Form, Midnight Form, and Dusk Form. Midday Form Midnight Form Dusk Form Midday Form [ edit | edit source ] Midday Form Lycanroc resembles a quadrupedal, light brown wolf. It has a white underbelly that extends up around its muzzle and eyes in a mask-like marking. It has pointed ears with dark brown tips and pink insides, big blue eyes, and a triangular, dark brown nose. There are four sharp fangs in its mouth: two in the upper jaw and two in the lower. Large tufts of brown fur extend from the sides of its head; three tufts on each side. A bushy, white mane covers its neck. Four dark brown rocks poke out of its mane, creating a shape similar to a stylized sun. The rocks are said to be razor-sharp. The lower portions of this form's legs are white, and each foot has three dark brown claws. It also has a long, fluffy white tail. Midday Form Lycanroc has a calm, collected, and cautious personality. In order to avoid needless fighting, it prefers to live alone and does not form a pack with others of its kind. However, it is a fiercely loyal partner to Trainers that accept it during its unruly stage. It also does whatever it can for those it cares about, and is known to use its claws and rocky mane to protect Rockruff in its care. In battle , it darts past opponents while attacking them with the rocks in its mane. These swift movements leave many foes bewildered and backed into a corner. [ 1 ] It always aims for the foes' weak spot using its fangs. Broken fragments of its stone mane are considered good luck. Concept art of Lycanroc's Midday Form Midnight Form [ edit | edit source ] In its Midnight Form, Lycanroc instead resembles a slouched, bipedal wolf or werewolf. It has short red fur with white on its lower legs and a white streak that covers its face, underbelly, and tail. It has pointed, button ears with black tips, three large tufts of fur on each side of its face, a triangular black nose, and a mouth full of sharp teeth. While the sclerae, irises, and pupils of its eye are visible, they all glow a pinkish red and no distinctive colors are visible. A mane of fur covers its back and shoulders, sweeping forward over its head to form a crescent moon shape. On either side of its body, just below the shoulders, two small rocks resembling claws poke out of the mane. A single, bigger stone projects out of the front of the mane over its head. Each of this form's paws has three black claws, and it has a stubby tail covered in long fur. Like the Midday Form, Lycanroc's Midnight Form is a solitary Pokémon, but it is thrilled by battling and becomes more excited the stronger its opponent. This excitement is what causes its eyes to glow. [ 2 ] Midnight Form Lycanroc is known to be reckless along with extremely vicious temper. Its strategy consists of closing in on its opponent and encouraging attack, then countering with a powerful blow that knocks out its opponent. The full force of its headbutts can destroy giant boulders. Pokémon Trainers that can draw out its power earn its trust, but it despises those that it disagrees with or who try to force it to fight. Concept art of Lycanroc's Midnight Form Dusk Form [ edit | edit source ] In its Dusk Form, Lycanroc resembles a combination of its Midday and Midnight Forms. Its body is similar to the Midday Form, with the same white color except with orange-gold fur. It retains the three brown claws for all four feet. The white fluffy mane resembles a sun with part of the crescent moon. It also has four rocks around its neck like a sun as well along with a single, bigger stone coming from the mane over its head. Its face resembles Midday Form Lycanroc's, but is orange-gold with bright green eyes, which can change to bright glowing red when angry or attacking. Dusk Form Lycanroc shares traits from the previous two forms. Its stance, calm demeanor, and loyal nature are shared with the Midday Form. However, it also shares the Midnight Form's fighting spirit, including the red, glowing eyes at the moment of attack. Dusk Form Lycanroc's fighting style and moveset is a mix of that of the Midday and Midnight Forms, [ 3 ] and it will relentlessly drive its opponents into a corner during a battle. Due to only evolving at sunset, this form of Lycanroc is considered to be the rarest. [ 4 ] It is rather difficult to train because of its temperamental nature, making it the most troublesome of the three forms. Unlike Midday and Midnight Form Lycanroc, it normally never barks. Concept art of Lycanroc's Dusk Form Lycanroc's exclusive Z-Move , Splintered Stormshards Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Lycanroc evolves from Rockruff . Rockruff can evolve into Midday Form, Midnight Form, or Dusk Form Lycanroc. (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Rockruff Rock → First Evolution Lycanroc Midday Form Rock → First Evolution Lycanroc Midnight Form Rock → First Evolution Lycanroc Dusk Form Rock ↑ 1.0 1.1 Lycanroc (Midday Form) | Pokémon Sun and Pokémon Moon ( archived ) ↑ 2.0 2.1 Lycanroc (Midnight Form) | Pokémon Sun and Pokémon Moon ( archived ) ↑ Lycanroc (Dusk Form) | Pokémon Ultra Sun and Pokémon Ultra Moon ( archvied ) ↑ Secrets of Lycanroc Dusk Form | Nintendo ( archived )

Generation VII Alola S M : #104 Alola US UM : #127 Kanto #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! . Midday Form Sun Its quick movements confuse its enemies. Well equipped with claws and fangs, it also uses the sharp rocks in its mane as weapons. Moon When properly raised from a young age, it will become a trustworthy partner that will absolutely never betray its Trainer . Ultra Sun The sharp rocks in its mane can cut like knives. Lycanroc wears its prey down by degrees before finishing them off. Ultra Moon The rocks in its mane are sharper than a knife. Fragments that break off are treasured as good luck charms. Midnight Form Sun The more intimidating the opponent it faces, the more this Pokémon 's blood boils. It will attack with no regard for its own safety. Moon It goads its enemies into attacking, withstands the hits, and in return, delivers a headbutt, crushing their bones with its rocky mane. Ultra Sun It has no problem ignoring orders it doesn't like. It doesn't seem to mind getting hurt at all—as long as it can finish off its opponent. Ultra Moon They live alone without forming packs. They will only listen to orders from Trainers who can draw out their true power. Dusk Form Ultra Sun Bathed in the setting sun of evening, Lycanroc has undergone a special kind of evolution. An intense fighting spirit underlies its calmness. Ultra Moon This strange form is the result of its evolving at dusk. It's very rare in Alola. Generation VIII Galar #— Isle of Armor #158 Crown Tundra #— Sinnoh #— Hisui #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Brilliant Diamond, Shining Pearl , and Legends: Arceus . Midday Form Sword This Lycanroc is calm and cautious. The rocks jutting from its mane are razor sharp. Shield With swift movements, this Pokémon gradually backs its prey into a corner. Lycanroc's fangs are always aimed toward opponents' weak spots. Midnight Form Sword This form of Lycanroc is reckless. It charges headlong at its opponents, attacking without any care about what injuries it might receive. Shield It's invigorated by powerful opponents–the stronger the better. A full-force headbutt from one of these Lycanroc can shatter giant boulders. Dusk Form Sword This form of Lycanroc is normally calm and quiet. Once a battle begins, however, this Pokémon displays a ferocious fighting spirit. Shield These Pokémon have both calm and ferocious qualities. Their temperamental nature makes them a difficult species to raise. Generation IX Paldea #90 Kitakami #71 Blueberry #— Lumiose #— Hyperspace #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Legends: Z-A . Midday Form Scarlet Lycanroc attacks its prey with its sharp claws and fangs. It loyally obeys the instructions of a Trainer it trusts. Violet It has a calm and collected demeanor. It swiftly closes in on its prey, then slices them with the rocks in its mane. Midnight Form Scarlet This Pokémon uses its rocky mane to slash any who approach. It will even disobey its Trainer if it dislikes the orders it was given. Violet This Lycanroc has an extremely vicious temperament. It will happily sustain injuries for the sake of taking down its opponent. Dusk Form Scarlet Normally this Pokémon doesn't even bark, but once it enters battle, it will relentlessly drive the opponent into a corner. Violet These Pokémon have both calm and ferocious qualities. It's said that this form of Lycanroc is the most troublesome to raise. Spin-off games Pokopia #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Pokopia . Bold indicates a move that gets STAB when used by Lycanroc Italic indicates a move that gets STAB only when used by an Evolution of Lycanroc Side game data [ edit | edit source ] Midday Form [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Shuffle Rock Attack Power: 60 - 135 20 #730 Rock Combo Increases damage done by any Rock types in the combo. PSB drops : Great Challenge (Week 7) Pokémon Rumble Rush Walking Speed: 1.25 seconds Base HP: 54 Base Attack: 79 Base Defense: 52 Base Speed: 70 New Pokémon Snap Photodex entry #134 This loyal Pokémon always does what it can for those it holds dear. It uses its rocky mane and claws to protect Rockruff in its care. Pokémon GO Base HP: 181 Base Attack: 231 Base Defense: 140 Egg Distance: N/A Buddy Distance: 5 km Evolution Requirement: N/A Fast Attacks: Rock Throw , Sucker Punch Charged Attacks: Stone Edge , Drill Run , Crunch , Trailblaze * Midnight Form [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Rumble Rush Walking Speed: 1.67 seconds Base HP: 56 Base Attack: 79 Base Defense: 54 Base Speed: 60 New Pokémon Snap Photodex entry #134 This proud, solitary Pokémon almost never eats anything that it didn’t catch itself. It’s always hungry for a battle , though—even against terribly strong opponents. Pokémon GO Base HP: 198 Base Attack: 218 Base Defense: 152 Egg Distance: N/A Buddy Distance: 5 km Evolution Requirement: N/A Fast Attacks: Rock Throw , Counter Charged Attacks: Stone Edge , Crunch , Psychic Fangs , Trailblaze * Dusk Form [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Rumble Rush File:Rush745DBody.png Walking Speed: 1.25 seconds Base HP: 54 Base Attack: 79 Base Defense: 52 Base Speed: 70 Pokémon GO Base HP: 181 Base Attack: 234 Base Defense: 139 Egg Distance: N/A Buddy Distance: 5 km Evolution Requirement: N/A Fast Attacks: Rock Throw , Sucker Punch , Counter Charged Attacks: Stone Edge , Crunch , Iron Head , Trailblaze Form data [ edit | edit source ] Lycanroc has three non-interchangeable forms: Midday Form, Midnight Form, and Dusk Form. Internally, the games consider Rockruff with Own Tempo to be a separate form of Rockruff with Keen Eye , Vital Spirit , or Steadfast , and Rockruff with Own Tempo and Dusk Form Lycanroc can breed to produce more Rockruff with Own Tempo. After initially being event -exclusive, Own Tempo Rockruff and Dusk Form Lycanroc were later made accessible within normal gameplay starting in Generation VIII . Lycanroc's forms have different base stats , Abilities , moves they can learn, and cries . Lycanroc Midday Form Rock Keen Eye or Sand Rush Hidden : Steadfast Evolve (during the day starting at level 25) HP Atk Def SpA SpD Spe 75 115 65 55 65 112 Lycanroc Midnight Form Rock Keen Eye or Vital Spirit Hidden : No Guard Evolve (at night starting at level 25) HP Atk Def SpA SpD Spe 85 115 75 55 75 82 Lycanroc Dusk Form Rock Tough Claws Evolve from Own Tempo Rockruff (during the evening starting at level 25) HP Atk Def SpA SpD Spe 75 117 65 55 65 110 Evolution data [ edit | edit source ] Lycanroc evolves from Rockruff starting at level 25. The form Rockruff evolves into depends on the time , as well as its Ability , which corresponds to one of Rockruff's two visually indistinct forms . Rockruff with the Abilities Keen Eye , Vital Spirit , or Steadfast evolve into Midday Form Lycanroc when leveled up during the day, and Midnight Form Lycanroc when leveled up at night. In Generation VII , Rockruff only evolves into Midday Form Lycanroc in Pokémon Sun and Ultra Sun , and Midnight Form Lycanroc in Pokémon Moon and Ultra Moon . Rockruff with the Ability Own Tempo is a separate form that can only evolve into Dusk Form Lycanroc. It evolves into this form when leveled up during the evening: from 5:00 to 5:59 PM in in-game time in Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon (equating to 5:00 to 5:59 PM in real-time in Ultra Sun and 5:00 to 5:59 AM in real-time in Ultra Moon), from 7:00 to 7:59 PM in real-time in Sword and Shield , and for a period lasting 3 minutes out of the 72-minute day/night cycle in Scarlet and Violet . Unevolved Rockruff Rock + Level 25 during the day → First Evolution Lycanroc Midday Form Rock + Level 25 at night → First Evolution Lycanroc Midnight Form Rock + Level 25 during the evening if its Ability is Own Tempo → First Evolution Lycanroc Dusk Form Rock Sprites [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation VII. Generation VII Sun Moon Ultra Sun Ultra Moon Front Back Front Back Front Back Generation VIII Sword Shield Cross-Generation HOME For other sprites and images, please see Lycanroc images on the Bulbagarden Archives .

sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation VII. Generation VII Alola S M : #104 Alola US UM : #127 Kanto #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! . Midday Form Sun Its quick movements confuse its enemies. Well equipped with claws and fangs, it also uses the sharp rocks in its mane as weapons. Moon When properly raised from a young age, it will become a trustworthy partner that will absolutely never betray its Trainer . Ultra Sun The sharp rocks in its mane can cut like knives. Lycanroc wears its prey down by degrees before finishing them off. Ultra Moon The rocks in its mane are sharper than a knife. Fragments that break off are treasured as good luck charms. Midnight Form Sun The more intimidating the opponent it faces, the more this Pokémon 's blood boils. It will attack with no regard for its own safety. Moon It goads its enemies into attacking, withstands the hits, and in return, delivers a headbutt, crushing their bones with its rocky mane. Ultra Sun It has no problem ignoring orders it doesn't like. It doesn't seem to mind getting hurt at all—as long as it can finish off its opponent. Ultra Moon They live alone without forming packs. They will only listen to orders from Trainers who can draw out their true power. Dusk Form Ultra Sun Bathed in the setting sun of evening, Lycanroc has undergone a special kind of evolution. An intense fighting spirit underlies its calmness. Ultra Moon This strange form is the result of its evolving at dusk. It's very rare in Alola. Generation VIII Galar #— Isle of Armor #158 Crown Tundra #— Sinnoh #— Hisui #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Brilliant Diamond, Shining Pearl , and Legends: Arceus . Midday Form Sword This Lycanroc is calm and cautious. The rocks jutting from its mane are razor sharp. Shield With swift movements, this Pokémon gradually backs its prey into a corner. Lycanroc's fangs are always aimed toward opponents' weak spots. Midnight Form Sword This form of Lycanroc is reckless. It charges headlong at its opponents, attacking without any care about what injuries it might receive. Shield It's invigorated by powerful opponents–the stronger the better. A full-force headbutt from one of these Lycanroc can shatter giant boulders. Dusk Form Sword This form of Lycanroc is normally calm and quiet. Once a battle begins, however, this Pokémon displays a ferocious fighting spirit. Shield These Pokémon have both calm and ferocious qualities. Their temperamental nature makes them a difficult species to raise. Generation IX Paldea #90 Kitakami #71 Blueberry #— Lumiose #— Hyperspace #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Legends: Z-A . Midday Form Scarlet Lycanroc attacks its prey with its sharp claws and fangs. It loyally obeys the instructions of a Trainer it trusts. Violet It has a calm and collected demeanor. It swiftly closes in on its prey, then slices them with the rocks in its mane. Midnight Form Scarlet This Pokémon uses its rocky mane to slash any who approach. It will even disobey its Trainer if it dislikes the orders it was given. Violet This Lycanroc has an extremely vicious temperament. It will happily sustain injuries for the sake of taking down its opponent. Dusk Form Scarlet Normally this Pokémon doesn't even bark, but once it enters battle, it will relentlessly drive the opponent into a corner. Violet These Pokémon have both calm and ferocious qualities. It's said that this form of Lycanroc is the most troublesome to raise. Spin-off games Pokopia #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Pokopia . Midday Form Lycanroc in the Alola Pokédex S M Midday Form Lycanroc in the Alola Pokédex US UM Midnight Form Lycanroc in the Alola Pokédex S M Midnight Form Lycanroc in the Alola Pokédex US UM Dusk Form Lycanroc in the Alola Pokédex US UM Midday Form Lycanroc in the Paldea Pokédex Midnight Form Lycanroc in the Kitakami Pokédex

sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry SM015 Rockruff and Lycanroc Rotom Pokédex Rockruff , the Puppy Pokémon. A Rock-type . Rockruff have lived with humans since ancient times. They train themselves by running around. Rockruff evolves into Lycanroc, which has two different forms based on when it evolves. It evolves into Midday Form Lycanroc during the day and Midnight Form Lycanroc during the night. Furthermore, when it gets close to evolving , Rockruff's temperament becomes more aggressive, and it tends to act more independent. It's common for it to disappear and then return after it has evolved.
